items.title: Champions League recap: Vinicius leads Madrid to win vs. Benfica, PSG with comeback
items.summary: Enjoy all that happened from today's knockout phase playoff first legs, including Benfica vs. Real Madrid and AS Monaco vs. Paris Saint-Germain.

items.title: Apparent racist abuse of Vinícius Júnior overshadows Real Madrid’s win in Benfica
items.summary: Vinícius Júnior and his teammates appeared ready to walk off and the game was put on hold for 10 minutes after the Brazilian told the referee that he had been subject to racist abuse after scoring the goal that gives Real Madrid a 1-0 first-leg lead in their Champions League playoff against Benfica. Vinícius had celebrated the strike dancing in the corner of the Estádio da Luz and in the confrontations that followed, Benfica’s Gianluca Prestianni said something to him while covering his mouth. Vinícius immediately ran to the referee, François Letexier, who stopped the match and crossed his arms to signal that he was doing so because of an accusation of racist abuse. Both managers came on to the pitch and Vinícius spoke to José Mourinho to explain what had happened. Conversations and confrontations continued as Vinícius sat alone on the bench, but eventually they restarted with the Brazilian on the pitch. The noise that accompanied Benfica’s attempts to get back into it, a red card for Mourinho as he protested a late foul on the edge of the Madrid area, unable to eclipse the feeling that there was something empty, something sadder, about the occasion now, like this match no longer mattered. Its lasting legacy will not be about the football. Continue reading...

items.title: Schreuder: 'Doorgaan op ingeslagen weg, ik maak Ajax-NEC niet groot'
items.summary: De strijd om plek twee is nog altijd in volle gang, al is Dick Schreuder daar naar eigen zeggen niet mee bezig. Na het gelijkspel van NEC op bezoek bij Sparta Rotterdam hebben de Nijmegenaren de derde plaats overgenomen van Ajax, de tegenstander komende zaterdag. De trainer van NEC blikt terug op het duel met Sparta, maar wordt ook gevraagd naar de wedstrijd van komend weekend tegen Ajax.

items.title: VI Live: Arbeloa kent beste start bij Real Madrid sinds Pellegrini
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam en NEC maakten dinsdagavond hun door de sneeuw geteisterde duel van afgelopen weekend af. Daarnaast ging de tussenronde van de Champions League van start, met onder meer Real Madrid, Paris Saint-Germain, Borussia Dortmund en Juventus. Lees de volledige avond terug in de VI Live.

items.title: Real Madrid wint bewogen CL-wedstrijd tegen Benfica, Mourinho mist return
items.summary: Real Madrid heeft deels revanche genomen op Benfica in de Champions League. De Koninklijke lukte dinsdagavond wat drie weken eerder niet lukte in Lissabon: winnen. Vinícius Júnior was in meerdere opzichten de hoofdrolspeler, net als José Mourinho.

items.title: Champions League-kanon Guirassy leidt Dortmund langs Atalanta en De Roon
items.summary: Borussia Dortmund heeft goede zaken gedaan in de tussenronde van de Champions League. De Duitse topclub rekende overtuigend af met Atalanta. Daardoor moet de ploeg van Marten de Roon in de return hopen op een stunt.

items.title: Vini Jr. reports racist abuse during Benfica-Madrid
items.summary: Real Madrid's Champions League playoff first leg at Benfica was paused for 10 minutes on Tuesday, after the referee initiated UEFA's anti-racism protocol following Vinícius Júnior's opening goal.

items.title: Balogun double sets U.S. mark in UCL knockouts
items.summary: American forward Folarin Balogun took less than one minute to give Monaco the lead against Paris Saint-Germain in their Champions League playoff on Tuesday.

items.title: Sources: Barça must pay Man Utd full Rashford fee
items.summary: Manchester United have no plans to re-negotiate the terms of Marcus Rashford's potential permanent move to Barcelona, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Inter's Bastoni sorry for celebrating Juve red card
items.summary: Inter Milan defender Alessandro Bastoni apologised for celebrating the controversial sending off of Juventus defender Pierre Kalulu's in Saturday's Derby d'Italia.

items.title: PSG overleeft rampstart in Monaco: Doué grijpt hoofdrol na uitvallen Dembélé
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain ligt op koers om de achtste finale van de Champions League te bereiken. De titelverdediger kwam woensdag op bezoek bij AS Monaco vroeg in de problemen, maar rechtte de rug en verliet Stade Louis II met een nipte overwinning.

items.title: Real Madrid tie halted over Vinicius racism allegation
items.summary: Real Madrid's Champions League knockout phase play-off tie at Benfica is halted for 10 minutes after Vinicius Junior reports alleged racist abuse.

items.title: Champions League roundup: Galatasaray hammer Juventus to seize control
items.summary: Juventus lead twice but go down 5-2 in playoff 1st leg Juan Cabal sent off at 3-2; Noa Lang and Gabriel Sara star Galatasaray delivered a dominant performance to dismantle 10-man Juventus 5-2 in the first leg of their Champions League playoff, with Noa Lang and Gabriel Sara starring in a scintillating display. The hosts took the lead in the 15th minute when the Brazilian Sara struck a thunderous long-range shot that left Michele Di Gregorio with no chance. However, Juventus equalised almost instantly when Teun Koopmeiners reacted quickly after Pierre Kalulu’s header was saved by Ugurcan Cakir, converting the rebound in the 16th minute. Continue reading...

items.title: Feyenoord training moved to Belgium for Sterling
items.summary: Feyenoord will train in Belgium this week instead of their home in Rotterdam so new signing Raheem Sterling can work with his new team-mates.

items.title: Arsenal announce rise in season-ticket prices for fifth consecutive season
items.summary: Arsenal Supporters’ Trust ‘disappointed’ by increase Club also confirm safe standing at Clock End next year Arsenal have announced that season-ticket prices will rise for a fifth consecutive season, with the cost of a ticket for the most dedicated fans rising by an average of 3.9%. The Premier League leaders, who face Wolves at Molineux on Wednesday, say they have made the decision as part of a continued push towards “financial stability” and confirmed that safe standing would be introduced to the Clock End next season, after consultation with supporters. Continue reading...

items.title: How key F1 meetings could have huge implications on 2026 season
items.summary: The final test of the 2026 Formula 1 pre-season gets under way on Wednesday, but the most crucial action of the day may well be away from the track at the Bahrain International Circuit.

items.title: Raya talks Arsenal's bid to win silverware and squad togetherness
items.summary: David Raya discusses his side's bid to win silverware this season and togetherness in the Gunners squad

items.title: Castleford 'heartbroken' as star signing suffers season-ending ACL injury
Australian player claims tennis is 'racist' and 'misogynistic' as she quits the sport
items.summary: Australia's Destanee Aiava has announced her retirement from tennis at the age of 25 and claims she is turning her back on the game's "racist, misogynistic, homophobic and hostile" culture.

items.title: Football Daily | Refereeing is stuck in a frustrating feedback loop, with no easy way out
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! It’s been another rough few days for Europe’s referees. On Saturday, Chris Kavanagh and his assistants had a night to forget at Villa Park without the comfort blanket of VAR. One poor decision is forgivable, two is careless, but there were a hat-trick of howlers, with Tammy Abraham clearly offside for Aston Villa’s opener. Lucas Digne needed his own dedicated review booth, escaping with a yellow card for an agricultural tackle on Jacob Murphy and avoiding a penalty for handball. Officials ruled that Digne handled the ball outside the penalty area, an impressive feat given that he both took off and landed inside the box as he jumped to block a cross. Kavanagh and one of his assistants have been stood down by PGMO, presumably rebranded because they can’t stop dropping Ls. Continue reading...
